question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open an unbounded number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() with no per-user connection limit, exhausting JVM heap memory through accumulation of Flux pipeline objects and TopicContext state, causing an OOM-based DoS for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user craft or trigger a stream of TopicMessage objects with consensusTimestamp values near Long.MIN_VALUE so that the division (topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000) produces extreme negative latency values, causing the Timer.record() call to be skipped but still flooding the doOnNext callback with arithmetic operations at high throughput, degrading CPU performance for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a high-volume message stream where every message has a consensusTimestamp such that latency > 0 is always true, causing getConsensusLatencyTimer().record() to be called millions of times per second, exhausting MeterRegistry internal data structures (ring buffers, histograms) and causing CPU saturation that denies service to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getConsensusLatencyTimer() (lazy Getter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in the Lombok-generated lazy @Getter for consensusLatencyTimer by opening thousands of concurrent subscriptions simultaneously, causing multiple Timer instances to be registered against the same MeterRegistry meter name 'hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency', resulting in a MeterRegistry duplicate-registration exception that crashes the listener pipeline for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime of 0 (epoch) so that filterMessage() never discards any message, causing the full unfiltered message stream from the underlying listener to be forwarded to the subscriber, amplifying memory and CPU usage proportionally to the total message volume in the system and causing DoS for co-located subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with limit=0 (no limit) and startTime=0, causing the Flux pipeline to never complete and hold a thread/scheduler slot indefinitely on Schedulers.boundedElastic(), and by repeating this across many connections exhaust the bounded elastic thread pool, causing a thread starvation DoS for all polling-mode subscribers?
